message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

before we get back to the work on the list, i have a question.  my friend is using CCUSAGE to count his token usage.  https://github.com/ccusage/ccusage . can we compare what that offers vs what we are doing now...?  also would ccusage work to count tokens on my monad system as well as from my openclaw fleet say if they were using codex / co-pilot etc.?

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- critic: Independent Critic review — quality governance for code changes
- janitor: Periodic codebase maintenance — systematic health check across VCS hygiene, code quality, documentation fitness, test coverage, dependencies, controllability, and more
- learnings: Look up project learnings and preferences relevant to your current task — returns only what matters, keeping context clean
- pr: PR lifecycle management — create, update, merge, or check status with independent reviewer
- prawduct-doctor: Product repo setup, health check, and repair
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Compare CCUSAGE token counting with current approach

content.text: I'll research this properly rather than answer from memory — let me pull the actual ccusage capabilities and look at how your current system counts tokens.

input.description: Map current token-counting system
input.prompt:
I need to understand how this portfolio project's stats/token-counting system works, so I can compare it against the third-party tool "ccusage".

Please investigate and report back concisely:

1. Find the stats collector code/scripts. Likely in a project-assets repo, a scripts/ dir, or referenced in the portfolio. Look for things that produce `_collect-meta.json`, `clawhub-versions.json`, or anything tracking "AI Tokens".
2. Specifically: HOW does it currently count AI tokens? What is the source of truth for token counts? (Memory notes mention: Monad system `tokens.total`, Antigravity/agy SQLite `gen_metadata` parse, a Gemini CLI parse, Volta double-count issues, "count tokens once at the router/source".)
3. What systems/fleets feed into it? (Monad, OpenClaw fleet, Antigravity, etc.) What token sources does each expose?
4. Where does the data ultimately get displayed (portfolio BuilderStats tiles)?

Report file paths and a plain-English summary of the token-counting data flow. Do NOT modify anything — read only. Be efficient; I need the architecture, not every line.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary report.

## Token-Counting System Architecture Report

Based on my investigation, here's how the portfolio's token-counting system works and how it differs from third-party tools like `ccusage`:

### **1. High-Level Data Flow**

```
Local Machines (Cursatory + habitat)
    ↓
    ├─ ccusage CLI (monthly, local Anthropic tokens)
    ├─ Gemini CLI telemetry (local logs)
    └─ Antigravity CLI SQLite (local conversation DBs)
         ↓
Local daily agent (~/.claude-stats/refresh.sh @ 05:30 PT)
         ↓
    Writes to project-assets:
    ├─ anthropic-usage.json
    ├─ gemini-usage.json
    └─ antigravity-usage.json
         ↓
GitHub stats collector (06:00 UTC daily)
         ↓
    Runs: tokens.mjs::aggregateTokens()
         ↓
    Merges 4 token sources per provider:
    ├─ Admin APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI)
    ├─ Local agent files (ccusage, Gemini, Antigravity)
    ├─ Manual static entries (projects.yml)
    └─ Prorated formulas (e.g., Copilot Free)
         ↓
Manifest (_collect-meta.json)
         ↓
    Contains:
    ├─ aggregateTokens.breakdown (per-provider)
    ├─ aggregateTokens.sources (e.g. "api+agent+manual")
    └─ aggregateTokens.total (sum of all)
         ↓
Portfolio (BuilderStats component)
         ↓
    Fetches manifest + Monad-1 stats (monad-stats.json)
    Sums: cloud tokens + local inference
    Displays: top "AI Tokens" tile
```

---

### **2. Token Sources & Counting Strategy**

#### **Cloud Providers (from `tokens.mjs`)**

| Provider | Primary Source | Fallback/Additional | Manual Override |
|----------|---|---|---|
| **Anthropic** | Admin API (unavailable for personal accounts) | ccusage local agent (`anthropic-usage.json`) | Manual entry in projects.yml |
| **OpenAI** | Admin API (organization/usage endpoint) | — | Manual entry (TypingMind prepaid) |
| **Copilot** | — (no per-user API) | Prorated formula (since 2026-03-01, ~1M/month) | — |
| **Cursor** | — (no API) | Static manual value (7.016B from CSV export 2025-05-04) | — |
| **Gemini** | — (sunsetted CLI) | Gemini CLI telemetry (`gemini-usage.json`) + Manual | Manual prepaid (305M) |
| **Antigravity** | — (successor to Gemini) | Local SQLite gen_metadata parse (`antigravity-usage.json`) | Manual entry |

#### **Local Inference (Monad-1)**

Separate flow: `monad-stats.json` (fetched from Monad-1 publisher)
- `tokens.total` = 212.5M (as of 2026-06-22 12:21:00Z)
- Includes ALL routed agent traffic:
  - LiteLLM proxy (:4000) — tracked internally
  - Ollama direct tee (:11435) — tracked internally
- **NOT added to cloud tokens** (double-count protection via `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS = []`)
- Verified: Volta agent's 61.4M matched Ollama tee's 61.5M ≈ already in Monad's total

---

### **3. Key Files & Paths**

| Path | Purpose | Last Updated |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `/Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets/` | Collector repo (moved from ~/Documents due to macOS TCC) | 2026-06-22 |
| `scripts/collect-stats.mjs` | Main orchestrator (runs daily 06:00 UTC) | Aggregates all stats |
| `scripts/lib/tokens.mjs` | Token aggregation logic (4 source types) | ~264 lines |
| `projects.yml` | Config: exclusions, overrides, token manual entries, prorated specs | Source of truth for static values |
| `_collect-meta.json` | Manifest (published to GitHub, consumed by portfolio) | ~26KB, updated daily |
| `monad-stats.json` | Local inference rig publisher output | ~4.5KB, pushed by Monad-1 |
| `anthropic-usage.json` | Local agent ccusage output (Cursatory + habitat) | 8.69B (2026-06-22) |
| `antigravity-usage.json` | Local agent Antigravity parse (successor to Gemini) | 6.1M (2026-06-22) |
| `local-agent/refresh.sh` | Daily cron script (05:30 PT on Cursatory) | Drives daily updates |

---

### **4. How Token Counting Works (Detailed)**

#### **At collection time (GitHub Actions, 06:00 UTC)**
```
aggregateTokens(projects.yml.tokens) →
  1. Fetch Anthropic admin API (or use 0 if key missing)
  2. Fetch OpenAI admin API (or use 0 if key missing)
  3. Read local agent files:
     - anthropic-usage.json (ccusage from Cursatory + habitat)
     - gemini-usage.json (Gemini CLI telemetry, sunsetted)
     - antigravity-usage.json (Antigravity CLI SQLite, new)
  4. Add manual static values from projects.yml:
     - anthropic: 60M (TypingMind prepaid)
     - openai: 35M (TypingMind prepaid)
     - cursor: 7.016B (static, user doesn't use anymore)
     - copilot: prorated (since 2026-03-01, ~1M/month)
  5. Apply prorated formulas:
     - Copilot: (days_since_start / 30.4375) * monthlyRate
  6. Sum per provider: api_total + agent_total + manual_total + prorated_total
  7. Return breakdown { anthropic, openai, copilot, cursor, gemini, antigravity }
```

#### **At portfolio display time (BuilderStats.jsx)**
```
1. Fetch manifest (_collect-meta.json from GitHub)
   → Extract: aggregateTokens.total (cloud providers)
2. Fetch Monad-1 stats (monad-stats.json)
   → Extract: tokens.total (local inference)
3. Sum: cloudTokens + monadTokens
4. Render on "AI Tokens" tile
5. Tooltip breakdown:
   - "Cloud providers: X.XXB" (from manifest)
   - "Monad-1 (local inference): Y.YYM" (from Monad publisher)
```

---

### **5. Comparison to `ccusage`**

**What `ccusage` does (tool you asked about):**
- CLI that queries local Anthropic Claude Code usage
- Outputs monthly breakdown in JSON
- Run-on-demand or as part of a cron job

**What this portfolio system does (different scope):**
- Aggregates tokens across **6 providers** (not just Anthropic)
- Adds **local inference** (Monad-1's self-published totals)
- Runs on a **daily schedule** (not manual invocation)
- Stores results in **GitHub** (accessible from portfolio app)
- **Merges 4 sources per provider**:
  1. Official admin APIs (most authoritative)
  2. Local agent tools (fallback for Anthropic, Gemini, Antigravity)
  3. Manual overrides (tools with no APIs, prepaid usage)
  4. Prorated formulas (estimated usage for free tiers)
- **Single source of truth** for the headline number

**Where ccusage fits in this system:**
- `ccusage monthly --json` is **source #2** for Anthropic
- Runs on Cursatory + habitat via the local refresh agent
- Output→ `anthropic-usage.json`→ Collected at 06:00 UTC
- Falls back to this if admin API is unavailable (it is for personal accounts)

---

### **6. Current Token Totals (as of 2026-06-22 12:30 UTC)**

**Manifest (cloud + prorated):** 15.7B total
- Anthropic: ~8.69B (8.69B from ccusage agent + 60M manual TypingMind)
- OpenAI: 35M+ (admin API if available + 35M manual TypingMind)
- Cursor: 7.016B (static, user deprecated)
- Gemini: ~305M (static manual, CLI sunsetted)
- Antigravity: 6.1M (new, Gemini CLI successor)
- Copilot: ~0.3M (prorated from 2026-03-01)

**Local inference (Monad-1):** 212.5M
- LiteLLM router: 151.1M
- Ollama direct tee: 61.4M

**Combined headline:** ~15.9B

---

### **7. Key Design Principles**

(From memory notes + code comments)

1. **Count once at the router** — Monad-1's `tokens.total` already includes all agents (Volta, etc.) routed to it. Never double-count by adding agents separately.

2. **Additive per provider** — Each provider's breakdown is the sum of api + agent + manual + prorated. No overlaps.

3. **No admin API for personal Anthropic accounts** — Falls back to ccusage local agent (most accurate available).

4. **Carry-forward guards** — If a data source fails (SSH to habitat down, Antigravity DBs pruned), compare to previous run and reject regressions >5%.

5. **Prorated is for free tiers** — Copilot Free estimated at ~1M/month; grows daily without manual bumps.

6. **TangleBrain future roadmap** — When TangleBrain's meter is live, it becomes the source of truth for local inference (replacing Monad-1's manual publisher).

---

### **File Paths for Reference**

- Portfolio manifest consumer: `/Users/jasonvaughan/Documents/Projects/JasonVaughanComPortfolio/src/components/BuilderStats.jsx`
- Token aggregator: `/Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets/scripts/lib/tokens.mjs`
- Config + manual values: `/Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets/projects.yml`
- Local agent orchestrator: `/Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets/local-agent/refresh.sh`
- Manifest (published output): `/Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets/_collect-meta.json`
- Monad-1 local stats: `/Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets/monad-stats.json`

- Runs on Cursatory + habitat via the local refresh agent
- Output→ `anthropic-usage.json`→ Collected at 06:00 UTC
- Falls back to this if admin API is unavailable (it is for personal accounts)

---

### **6. Current Token Totals (as of 2026-06-22 12:30 UTC)**

**Manifest (cloud + prorated):** 15.7B total
- Anthropic: ~8.69B (8.69B from ccusage agent + 60M manual TypingMind)
- OpenAI: 35M+ (admin API if available + 35M manual TypingMind)
- Cursor: 7.016B (static, user deprecated)
- Gemini: ~305M (static manual, CLI sunsetted)
- Antigravity: 6.1M (new, Gemini CLI successor)
- Copilot: ~0.3M (prorated from 2026-03-01)

**Local inference (Monad-1):** 212.5M
- LiteLLM router: 151.1M
- Ollama direct tee: 61.4M

**Combined headline:** ~15.9B

---

### **7. Key Design Principles**

(From memory notes + code comments)

1. **Count once at the router** — Monad-1's `tokens.total` already includes all agents (Volta, etc.) routed to it. Never double-count by adding agents separately.

2. **Additive per provider** — Each provider's breakdown is the sum of api + agent + manual + prorated. No overlaps.

3. **No admin API for personal Anthropic accounts** — Falls back to ccusage local agent (most accurate available).

4. **Carry-forward guards** — If a data source fails (SSH to habitat down, Antigravity DBs pruned), compare to previous run and reject regressions >5%.

5. **Prorated is for free tiers** — Copilot Free estimated at ~1M/month; grows daily without manual bumps.

6. **TangleBrain future roadmap** — When TangleBrain's meter is live, it becomes the source of truth for local inference (replacing Monad-1's manual publisher).

---

Does it support: Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, GitHub Copilot, Gemini, Bedrock, Vertex, or any other agents? How does it get the token counts (does it read local JSONL/log files, or call APIs)? Does it work offline from local files? List any subcommands (daily, monthly, session, blocks, etc.) and any MCP server feature.

content.content:
# ccusage Overview

## Purpose
ccusage analyzes coding agent CLI token usage and costs from local data, providing daily, weekly, monthly, and session reports.

## Supported Data Sources
ccusage reads local usage data from these agents:
- Claude Code
- Codex
- OpenCode
- Amp
- Droid
- Codebuff
- Hermes Agent
- pi-agent
- Goose
- OpenClaw
- Kilo
- Kimi
- Qwen
- GitHub Copilot CLI
- Gemini CLI

**Notable gaps:** No support for OpenAI's primary APIs, AWS Bedrock, or Google Vertex AI.

## Data Collection Method
The tool reads **local files** rather than calling APIs. It accesses agent CLI log/data files stored on your system, making it fully functional offline using pre-cached pricing data with the `--offline` flag.

## Subcommands
- `daily` — aggregated by date
- `weekly` — aggregated by week
- `monthly` — aggregated by month
- `session` — grouped by conversation sessions
- `blocks` — tracks Claude's 5-hour billing windows
- `statusline` — compact usage display for status bar integration (Beta)

## MCP Server Feature
The documentation mentions ".mcp.json" configuration file in the repository structure, suggesting MCP server integration capability, though specific details aren't elaborated in the provided content.

## Key Features
Supports model tracking, cost breakdown, date filtering, custom pricing overrides, and JSON export functionality.

content.text:
Here's the comparison. The headline surprise: **you're already using ccusage** — it's source #2 for your Anthropic numbers (your local agent runs `ccusage` on Cursatory + habitat, dumps to `anthropic-usage.json`, and the collector picks it up). So this isn't "ccusage vs. your system" — it's "should you lean on ccusage for *more* than just Anthropic." Let me break it down.

## What ccusage actually is

A read-only CLI that parses **local log files** that coding-agent CLIs write to disk, then aggregates them into daily/weekly/monthly/session reports with cost estimates. It does **not** call provider APIs (except for cached pricing data — it even has an `--offline` flag). No file on disk → no data.

Crucially, the list of agents it now reads from is much bigger than just Claude Code:

> Claude Code, **Codex**, OpenCode, Amp, Droid, Codebuff, Hermes, pi-agent, Goose, **OpenClaw**, Kilo, Kimi, Qwen, **GitHub Copilot CLI**, **Gemini CLI**

## ccusage vs. your collector

| | ccusage | Your collector (`tokens.mjs`) |
|---|---|---|
| **Scope** | One machine's local agent-CLI logs | 6 cloud providers + Monad local inference, fleet-wide |
| **How it counts** | Parses local JSONL/log files | 4 merged sources/provider: admin APIs, local-agent files (incl. ccusage), manual `projects.yml` entries, prorated formulas |
| **Providers w/o a CLI log** | Can't see them (no Cursor static, no TypingMind prepaid, no admin-API pulls) | Handles via manual/prorated entries |
| **Local inference (Monad)** | ❌ doesn't read LiteLLM/Ollama server logs | ✅ via Monad's own `tokens.total` publisher |
| **Output** | Terminal/JSON, on-demand | Daily GitHub-published manifest the portfolio reads |
| **Double-count guards** | None (it just sums logs) | Yes — `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS=[]`, carry-forward regression checks |

Bottom line: ccusage is a **better feeder for the agent-CLI side** than what you'd hand-roll, but it's a *component*, not a replacement. Your collector is the aggregation/dedup/publish layer it can't be.

## Would ccusage count Monad?

**No — not the way you'd want.** Monad is the *inference rig* (Ollama + LiteLLM router). ccusage reads the **client/agent side** (what a coding-CLI logged locally), never the model-server side. It has no parser for LiteLLM or Ollama server logs. Monad's own `tokens.total` publisher is the correct source, and that's already what you use.

Worse, if you pointed ccusage at agents that *route to* Monad, you'd **double-count** — those tokens are already inside Monad's `tokens.total`. That's the exact trap your `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS=[]` guard exists to prevent.

## Would ccusage count the OpenClaw fleet (Codex / Copilot / etc.)?

**Yes — this is the real opportunity.** ccusage explicitly supports **OpenClaw, Codex, and GitHub Copilot CLI** as first-class sources now. Those are providers your collector currently handles weakly:
- **Copilot** — today it's a *prorated guess* (~1M/month). ccusage would read the actual Copilot CLI logs → real numbers.
- **Codex** — not really tracked today; ccusage would give you genuine usage.
- **OpenClaw** — ccusage can read its logs directly.

**The one catch:** ccusage is local-file-based, so it has to run *where the logs live*. For a distributed fleet you'd either run ccusage on each node and aggregate the JSON, or rsync the log dirs to one box. But — you already do exactly this pattern: `local-agent/refresh.sh` runs ccusage across Cursatory + habitat and writes `anthropic-usage.json`. Extending it to emit `codex-usage.json` / `copilot-usage.json` from the same ccusage invocations (it's one tool, many sources) is a small, natural step.

And you'd still need the dedup discipline: if an OpenClaw/Codex agent routes through Monad, its tokens are already in Monad's total — count it in *one* place.

## Recommendation

Don't replace anything. **Widen your existing ccusage usage** from Anthropic-only to also emit Codex + Copilot CLI numbers, replacing the prorated Copilot guess with real data. Keep Monad on its own publisher, keep the collector as the dedup/aggregation/publish brain.

Want me to file a GitHub issue in `project-assets` to track "extend local-agent ccusage pull to Codex + Copilot CLI sources, retire the Copilot prorated estimate"? That's the concrete next step, and it fits your file-an-issue-before-fixing convention.

content: You asked how ccusage compares to your token-counting system and whether it'd work for Monad and the OpenClaw fleet. I explained you already use ccusage for Anthropic; it can't count Monad but can count Codex/Copilot. Next: file an issue to widen ccusage to those sources?

message.content: can you give me a table on how we are counting the tokens now including showing what CCUSAGE is doing in the scheme

content.text:
Here's the full picture — a per-provider table showing every source feeding your token count, with ccusage's role called out explicitly.

## How tokens are counted today

| Provider | Primary source | ccusage's role | Manual / prorated | Current value |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Anthropic** | Admin API *(unavailable for personal accounts → falls back)* | ✅ **This is where ccusage lives.** Runs on Cursatory + habitat → `anthropic-usage.json` → collector source #2 | + 60M manual (TypingMind prepaid) | ~8.69B |
| **OpenAI** | Admin API (org usage endpoint) | ❌ ccusage can't (no API support) | + 35M manual (TypingMind prepaid) | 35M+ |
| **Copilot** | — (no per-user API) | ⚠️ **ccusage *could* do this** (supports Copilot CLI) — but not wired in yet | Prorated formula (~1M/mo since 2026-03-01) | ~0.3M |
| **Cursor** | — (no API) | ❌ no Cursor CLI log support | Static 7.016B (CSV export 2025-05-04, deprecated) | 7.016B |
| **Gemini** | — (CLI sunsetted) | Gemini CLI telemetry → `gemini-usage.json` *(separate parse, not ccusage)* | + 305M manual prepaid | ~305M |
| **Antigravity** | — (Gemini successor) | ❌ Antigravity SQLite `gen_metadata` parse → `antigravity-usage.json` *(custom, not ccusage)* | manual entry | 6.1M |
| **Local inference (Monad-1)** | Monad's own `tokens.total` publisher → `monad-stats.json` | ❌ ccusage can't read LiteLLM/Ollama server logs | — (LiteLLM 151.1M + Ollama tee 61.4M) | 212.5M |

**Headline "AI Tokens" tile** = cloud manifest total (~15.7B) **+** Monad-1 local inference (212.5M) ≈ **~15.9B**

## Where ccusage sits in the data flow

```
LOCAL MACHINES (Cursatory + habitat)
  │
  ├─ ccusage CLI ──────────────► anthropic-usage.json   ◄── ccusage's ONLY current job
  ├─ Gemini CLI telemetry ─────► gemini-usage.json
  └─ Antigravity SQLite parse ─► antigravity-usage.json
        │
        ▼
  local-agent/refresh.sh  (05:30 PT daily, on Cursatory)
        │  pushes the *-usage.json files to project-assets
        ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ GITHUB COLLECTOR — tokens.mjs::aggregateTokens()         │
│ (06:00 UTC daily)                                        │
│                                                          │
│ Per provider, sums up to 4 source types:                 │
│   1. Admin APIs        (Anthropic, OpenAI)               │
│   2. Local-agent files (ccusage, Gemini, Antigravity) ◄──┼── ccusage feeds here
│   3. Manual static     (projects.yml: TypingMind, Cursor)│
│   4. Prorated formula  (Copilot ~1M/mo)                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │
        ▼
  _collect-meta.json  (published manifest, ~26KB)
        │
        ▼                        ┌─ monad-stats.json (Monad-1 publisher, separate)
  PORTFOLIO — BuilderStats.jsx ──┤
        │   cloudTokens + monadTokens
        ▼
  "AI Tokens" tile (~15.9B)
```

## The one-line summary

ccusage is doing exactly **one** job in your stack today: turning Claude Code's local logs on Cursatory + habitat into the Anthropic number, as a fallback because personal accounts have no admin API. Everything else (OpenAI API, Cursor static, Gemini telemetry, Antigravity SQLite, Copilot proration, Monad inference) is handled by *other* sources — and the collector is what merges, dedups, and publishes them.

The gap the earlier analysis flagged is visible right in the table: the **Copilot** row is a guess (prorated), and ccusage already supports the Copilot CLI — so that's the obvious place to put ccusage to work next.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about `@ccusage/codex`:

## Package Status

@ccusage/codex is at version 19.0.0, but the package has been deprecated in favor of npx ccusage (use ccusage@20).

## Installation & Usage

You can install @ccusage/codex in your project by running `npm i @ccusage/codex`. Alternatively, you can run it directly without installation:

```bash
npx @ccusage/codex@latest
bunx @ccusage/codex@latest  # Note: @latest is required for bunx
```

## CLI Usage Examples

For daily Codex usage, use `ccusage codex daily`, and for monthly Codex usage, use `ccusage codex monthly`.

By default, ccusage codex uses --speed auto, reads config.toml from each CODEX_HOME root, and treats service_tier = "priority" or legacy service_tier = "fast" as fast pricing when any configured root opts into it.

Speed options include:
- `ccusage codex daily --speed auto` (default)
- `ccusage codex daily --speed fast`
- `ccusage codex daily --speed standard`

## What It Does

ccusage can read OpenAI Codex CLI session logs and uses the same unified report model as Claude Code, OpenCode, Amp, Droid, Codebuff, Hermes Agent, and other AI coding tools.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Now it's clear. **Codex support ships as `@ccusage/codex`** (the `ccusage codex` subcommand), and you have **real Codex session data** on disk (`~/.codex/sessions/2026/06/...`, recent). OpenClaw I'll treat carefully — those agents likely route through Monad, whose `tokens.total` already counts them (double-count risk, the exact thing your `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS=[]` guard prevents).

aiTitle: Compare CCUSAGE token counting with current approach

content.text: Collector code is clear now. The pattern for adding a source is clean and well-established (agent file → breakdown → sources → agentTotal). The **one thing that decides whether Codex is safe to add** is double-counting: your OpenAI number comes from the OpenAI admin API, and Codex CLI can auth two ways — if it's using an **API key**, its tokens are *already* in that admin-API number (double-count); if it's using **ChatGPT login**, they're not (safe to add). Let me settle that and confirm the right ccusage invocation.

content.text:
Decisive results:

- **Codex auth_mode = `chatgpt`** (ChatGPT login, no API key). That means Codex usage is billed through the ChatGPT subscription, **not** the OpenAI API platform — so it's **not** in your OpenAI admin-API number. **Purely additive, zero double-count.** This is the clean win.
- **`npx ccusage@latest codex monthly --json` works** and returns real data (gpt-5.4 sessions). The installed 20.0.14 lacks the subcommand, but `@latest` has it — same `npx -y ccusage@latest` pattern your habitat path already uses.
- **OpenClaw**: deferring — those route through Monad (double-count). **Copilot**: no logs on this machine — keep prorated.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Daily refresh of Anthropic Claude Code token totals from Cursatory + habitat,
3	# pushed as anthropic-usage.json into the project-assets repo for the
4	# centralized stats collector to consume.
5	#
6	# Triggered by ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.jasonvaughan.claude-stats.plist
7	# (daily at 05:30 local). May also be run manually for testing.
8	#
9	# NOTE: the clone lives in ~/code (NOT ~/Documents). macOS TCC blocks launchd
10	# agents from writing into ~/Documents/Desktop/Downloads without Full Disk
11	# Access, which silently froze this file at 2026-04-28 ("Operation not
12	# permitted" on the write). Keep this path outside the TCC-protected folders.
13	
14	set -euo pipefail
15	
16	PROJECT_ASSETS="$HOME/code/project-assets"
17	USAGE_FILE="$PROJECT_ASSETS/anthropic-usage.json"
18	GEMINI_USAGE_FILE="$PROJECT_ASSETS/gemini-usage.json"
19	GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG="$HOME/.gemini/telemetry.log"
20	LOG_DIR="$HOME/.claude-stats"
21	LOG_FILE="$LOG_DIR/last-run.log"
22	# Antigravity (agy) — successor to the sunsetted Gemini CLI. Usage is read from
23	# agy's local SQLite conversation DBs (one global store, launcher-independent, so
24	# terminal/cron/TangleClaw sessions all count) via a hardened protobuf parser.
25	ANTIGRAVITY_USAGE_FILE="$PROJECT_ASSETS/antigravity-usage.json"
26	ANTIGRAVITY_CONV_DIR="$HOME/.gemini/antigravity-cli/conversations"
27	ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER="$LOG_DIR/antigravity-tokens.py"
28	
29	# Make sure the npm-global bin is on PATH (where ccusage is installed locally).
30	export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin"
31	
32	mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
33	exec > >(tee "$LOG_FILE") 2>&1
34	
35	echo "=== Claude Code stats refresh: $(date -u +%FT%TZ) ==="
36	
37	# Helper: parse ccusage JSON output (strips ANSI, sums totalTokens across months)
38	parse_total() {
39	  python3 -c "
40	import sys, json, re
41	raw = sys.stdin.read()
42	clean = re.sub(r'\x1b\[[0-9;]*m', '', raw)
43	start = clean.find('{')
44	end = clean.rfind('}')
45	if start == -1:
46	    print(0); sys.exit(0)
47	try:
48	    data = json.loads(clean[start:end+1])
49	    print(sum(m.get('totalTokens', 0) for m in data.get('monthly', [])))
50	except Exception:
51	    print(0)
52	"
53	}
54	
55	# Cursatory (local)
56	echo "[cursatory] running ccusage..."
57	CURSATORY_TOTAL=$(ccusage monthly --json 2>/dev/null | parse_total)
58	echo "[cursatory] total: $CURSATORY_TOTAL"
59	
60	# habitat (via SSH; uses npx since ccusage isn't installed globally there)
61	echo "[habitat] running ccusage via SSH..."
62	HABITAT_TOTAL=$(ssh -o ConnectTimeout=15 -o BatchMode=yes habitat \
63	  'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; npx -y ccusage@latest monthly --json' 2>/dev/null \
64	  | parse_total) || HABITAT_TOTAL=0
65	echo "[habitat] total: $HABITAT_TOTAL"
66	
67	TOTAL=$((CURSATORY_TOTAL + HABITAT_TOTAL))
68	echo "[combined] total: $TOTAL"
69	
70	if [[ "$TOTAL" -eq 0 ]]; then
71	  echo "ERROR: combined total is 0; refusing to write zero-value usage file."
72	  exit 1
73	fi
74	
75	# Regression guard: if either machine was unreachable, the partial total
76	# would silently regress the live number. Compare to last successful run.
77	PREV_TOTAL=0
78	if [[ -f "$USAGE_FILE" ]]; then
79	  PREV_TOTAL=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(open('$USAGE_FILE')).get('total', 0))" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
80	fi
81	if [[ "$PREV_TOTAL" -gt 0 ]]; then
82	  THRESHOLD=$((PREV_TOTAL * 95 / 100))
83	  if [[ "$TOTAL" -lt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
84	    echo "ERROR: new total $TOTAL is >5% below previous $PREV_TOTAL — likely a machine was unreachable."
85	    echo "  Refusing to overwrite. Will retry on the next scheduled run."
86	    exit 2
87	  fi
88	fi
89	
90	NOW=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
91	
92	cat > "$USAGE_FILE" <<EOF
93	{
94	  "total": $TOTAL,
95	  "byMachine": {
96	    "cursatory": $CURSATORY_TOTAL,
97	    "habitat": $HABITAT_TOTAL
98	  },
99	  "fetchedAt": "$NOW"
100	}
101	EOF
102	
103	echo "[file] wrote $USAGE_FILE"
104	
105	# === Gemini CLI telemetry → gemini-usage.json ===
106	# Telemetry is enabled in ~/.gemini/settings.json with target=local. Each
107	# Gemini CLI session appends OTLP-like JSON events to telemetry.log; we sum
108	# input + output + cached token counts across all gemini_cli.api_response
109	# events. If the log doesn't exist yet, write a zero-value file (Medusa is
110	# the only project using Gemini CLI today; log populates on next session).
111	
112	# Incremental parse: telemetry.log grows without bound (it reached 70GB once a
113	# full-file scan was reattempted), so we never re-read history. We track a byte
114	# offset in $GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE and only sum tokens from NEW bytes each run,
115	# carrying the cumulative total forward in $GEMINI_USAGE_FILE. First run (no
116	# offset file) baselines at EOF — historical pre-fix telemetry is not
117	# retro-counted (it was never in the headline; the manifest used a manual value).
118	GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE="$LOG_DIR/gemini-offset"
119	GEMINI_TOTAL=0
120	if [[ -f "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" ]]; then
121	  echo "[gemini] incremental parse of $GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG..."
122	  GEMINI_TOTAL=$(python3 -c "
123	import os, re, json
124	log = '$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG'
125	offset_file = '$GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE'
126	usage_file = '$GEMINI_USAGE_FILE'
127	
128	prev_total = 0
129	try:
130	    with open(usage_file) as f:
131	        prev_total = int(json.load(f).get('total', 0))
132	except Exception:
133	    prev_total = 0
134	
135	size = os.path.getsize(log)
136	try:
137	    with open(offset_file) as f:
138	        start = int(f.read().strip())
139	except Exception:
140	    start = None
141	
142	if start is None:
143	    start = size          # first run: skip history, count only new telemetry
144	elif start > size:
145	    start = 0             # log rotated/truncated: re-scan the smaller file
146	
147	delta = 0
148	pat = re.compile(r'(input_token_count|output_token_count|cached_content_token_count|tool_token_count)[\":=\s]+([0-9]+)')
149	with open(log, 'r', errors='replace') as f:
150	    f.seek(start)
151	    f.readline()          # discard a possibly-partial line at the seek boundary
152	    for line in f:
153	        for m in pat.finditer(line):
154	            try:
155	                delta += int(m.group(2))
156	            except ValueError:
157	                pass
158	
159	with open(offset_file, 'w') as f:
160	    f.write(str(size))
161	print(prev_total + delta)
162	" 2>/dev/null)
163	  GEMINI_TOTAL=${GEMINI_TOTAL:-0}
164	  echo "[gemini] total: $GEMINI_TOTAL (cumulative, incremental)"
165	else
166	  echo "[gemini] no telemetry log yet at $GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG (will populate after next CLI session)"
167	fi
168	
169	# Rolling size cap: keep the newest GEMINI_LOG_CAP_GB, prune oldest. Runs AFTER
170	# the incremental parse (which counted through EOF and saved the offset), so
171	# dropping already-counted old bytes loses nothing; we reset the offset to the
172	# post-trim EOF so the next run counts only new appends. Gemini CLI itself has
173	# no rotation (settings.json is just enabled/target/outfile) and re-logs its full
174	# ~39KB system prompt on every event, so without this the log grows unbounded.
175	# Hysteresis: prune only ABOVE the high watermark, and trim down to the low one —
176	# so a few MB over cap doesn't rewrite 10GB every run (that thrash blocked the rest
177	# of the script). Gemini CLI is sunsetted now, so this log is near-static; this is
178	# just a cheap safety backstop, not a daily chore.
179	GEMINI_LOG_HIGH_GB=12
180	GEMINI_LOG_LOW_GB=8
181	if [[ -f "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" ]]; then
182	  HIGH_BYTES=$(( GEMINI_LOG_HIGH_GB * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 ))
183	  LOW_BYTES=$(( GEMINI_LOG_LOW_GB * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 ))
184	  CUR_BYTES=$(stat -f%z "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
185	  if (( CUR_BYTES > HIGH_BYTES )); then
186	    echo "[gemini] log $(( CUR_BYTES / 1073741824 ))GB > ${GEMINI_LOG_HIGH_GB}GB — pruning oldest down to ${GEMINI_LOG_LOW_GB}GB..."
187	    TRIM_TMP="${GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG}.trim.$$"
188	    if tail -c "$LOW_BYTES" "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" > "$TRIM_TMP" 2>/dev/null && mv "$TRIM_TMP" "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG"; then
189	      stat -f%z "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" > "$GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE"
190	      echo "[gemini] trimmed to $(( $(stat -f%z "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG") / 1073741824 ))GB; offset reset to new EOF"
191	    else
192	      rm -f "$TRIM_TMP"
193	      echo "[gemini] WARN: trim failed; log left intact"
194	    fi
195	  fi
196	fi
197	
198	cat > "$GEMINI_USAGE_FILE" <<EOF
199	{
200	  "total": $GEMINI_TOTAL,
201	  "source": "gemini-cli local telemetry",
202	  "telemetryLog": "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG",
203	  "fetchedAt": "$NOW"
204	}
205	EOF
206	echo "[file] wrote $GEMINI_USAGE_FILE"
207	
208	# === Antigravity (agy) usage → antigravity-usage.json ===
209	# Cumulative lifetime tokens summed from agy's local SQLite conversation DBs,
210	# cached INCLUDED. Carry-forward guard: if the recomputed total drops below the
211	# last recorded value (e.g. agy prunes old conversation DBs), keep the previous
212	# higher value so the lifetime stat never regresses.
213	if [[ -f "$ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER" ]]; then
214	  echo "[antigravity] parsing $ANTIGRAVITY_CONV_DIR ..."
215	  AGY_JSON=$(python3 "$ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER" "$ANTIGRAVITY_CONV_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
216	  if [[ -n "$AGY_JSON" ]]; then
217	    if python3 - "$ANTIGRAVITY_USAGE_FILE" "$NOW" "$AGY_JSON" <<'PYEOF'
218	import json, sys
219	usage_file, now, agy_json = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]
220	new = json.loads(agy_json)
221	prev_total = 0
222	try:
223	    with open(usage_file) as f:
224	        prev_total = int(json.load(f).get('total', 0))
225	except Exception:
226	    prev_total = 0
227	total = int(new.get('total', 0))
228	if total < prev_total:
229	    print(f"[antigravity] WARN: recomputed {total:,} < previous {prev_total:,} (DBs pruned?); keeping previous", file=sys.stderr)
230	    sys.exit(0)
231	out = {
232	    "total": total,
233	    "prompt": int(new.get('prompt', 0)),
234	    "output": int(new.get('output', 0)),
235	    "cached": int(new.get('cached', 0)),
236	    "tool": int(new.get('tool', 0)),
237	    "includesCached": True,
238	    "source": "antigravity-cli local SQLite gen_metadata",
239	    "dbs": int(new.get('dbs', 0)),
240	    "rows": int(new.get('rows', 0)),
241	    "fetchedAt": now,
242	}
243	with open(usage_file, 'w') as f:
244	    json.dump(out, f, indent=2)
245	print(f"[antigravity] total {total:,} (prompt {out['prompt']:,} + output {out['output']:,} + cached {out['cached']:,} + tool {out['tool']:,})")
246	PYEOF
247	    then :; else echo "[antigravity] WARN: writer failed — leaving previous antigravity-usage.json"; fi
248	  else
249	    echo "[antigravity] parser returned nothing — skipping (no DBs yet?)"
250	  fi
251	else
252	  echo "[antigravity] parser not found at $ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER — skipping"
253	fi
254	
255	cd "$PROJECT_ASSETS"
256	# Stage + commit BEFORE pulling: the script writes the usage files first, so a
257	# pull --rebase against a dirty tree errors ("unstaged changes"). Commit first,
258	# then rebase our commit onto the latest remote (the cloud collector pushes
259	# several times a day), then push. --autostash covers any stray local edits.
260	git add anthropic-usage.json gemini-usage.json
261	if [[ -f antigravity-usage.json ]]; then git add antigravity-usage.json; fi
262	if git diff --cached --quiet; then
263	  echo "[git] no changes to commit"
264	  git pull --rebase --autostash --quiet || true
265	else
266	  git commit -m "chore(stats): refresh AI usage agents ($NOW)"
267	  git pull --rebase --autostash --quiet
268	  git push --quiet

270	fi
271	
272	echo "=== done ==="

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Daily refresh of Anthropic Claude Code token totals from Cursatory + habitat,
# pushed as anthropic-usage.json into the project-assets repo for the
# centralized stats collector to consume.
#
# Triggered by ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.jasonvaughan.claude-stats.plist
# (daily at 05:30 local). May also be run manually for testing.
#
# NOTE: the clone lives in ~/code (NOT ~/Documents). macOS TCC blocks launchd
# agents from writing into ~/Documents/Desktop/Downloads without Full Disk
# Access, which silently froze this file at 2026-04-28 ("Operation not
# permitted" on the write). Keep this path outside the TCC-protected folders.

set -euo pipefail

PROJECT_ASSETS="$HOME/code/project-assets"
USAGE_FILE="$PROJECT_ASSETS/anthropic-usage.json"
GEMINI_USAGE_FILE="$PROJECT_ASSETS/gemini-usage.json"
GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG="$HOME/.gemini/telemetry.log"
LOG_DIR="$HOME/.claude-stats"
LOG_FILE="$LOG_DIR/last-run.log"
# Antigravity (agy) — successor to the sunsetted Gemini CLI. Usage is read from
# agy's local SQLite conversation DBs (one global store, launcher-independent, so
# terminal/cron/TangleClaw sessions all count) via a hardened protobuf parser.
ANTIGRAVITY_USAGE_FILE="$PROJECT_ASSETS/antigravity-usage.json"
ANTIGRAVITY_CONV_DIR="$HOME/.gemini/antigravity-cli/conversations"
ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER="$LOG_DIR/antigravity-tokens.py"

# Make sure the npm-global bin is on PATH (where ccusage is installed locally).
export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin"

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
exec > >(tee "$LOG_FILE") 2>&1

echo "=== Claude Code stats refresh: $(date -u +%FT%TZ) ==="

# Helper: parse ccusage JSON output (strips ANSI, sums totalTokens across months)
parse_total() {
  python3 -c "
import sys, json, re
raw = sys.stdin.read()
clean = re.sub(r'\x1b\[[0-9;]*m', '', raw)
start = clean.find('{')
end = clean.rfind('}')
if start == -1:
    print(0); sys.exit(0)
try:
    data = json.loads(clean[start:end+1])
    print(sum(m.get('totalTokens', 0) for m in data.get('monthly', [])))
except Exception:
    print(0)
"
}

# Cursatory (local)
echo "[cursatory] running ccusage..."
CURSATORY_TOTAL=$(ccusage monthly --json 2>/dev/null | parse_total)
echo "[cursatory] total: $CURSATORY_TOTAL"

# habitat (via SSH; uses npx since ccusage isn't installed globally there)
echo "[habitat] running ccusage via SSH..."
HABITAT_TOTAL=$(ssh -o ConnectTimeout=15 -o BatchMode=yes habitat \
  'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; npx -y ccusage@latest monthly --json' 2>/dev/null \
  | parse_total) || HABITAT_TOTAL=0
echo "[habitat] total: $HABITAT_TOTAL"

TOTAL=$((CURSATORY_TOTAL + HABITAT_TOTAL))
echo "[combined] total: $TOTAL"

if [[ "$TOTAL" -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: combined total is 0; refusing to write zero-value usage file."
  exit 1
fi

# Regression guard: if either machine was unreachable, the partial total
# would silently regress the live number. Compare to last successful run.
PREV_TOTAL=0
if [[ -f "$USAGE_FILE" ]]; then
  PREV_TOTAL=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(open('$USAGE_FILE')).get('total', 0))" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
fi
if [[ "$PREV_TOTAL" -gt 0 ]]; then
  THRESHOLD=$((PREV_TOTAL * 95 / 100))
  if [[ "$TOTAL" -lt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: new total $TOTAL is >5% below previous $PREV_TOTAL — likely a machine was unreachable."
    echo "  Refusing to overwrite. Will retry on the next scheduled run."
    exit 2
  fi
fi

NOW=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

cat > "$USAGE_FILE" <<EOF
{
  "total": $TOTAL,
  "byMachine": {
    "cursatory": $CURSATORY_TOTAL,
    "habitat": $HABITAT_TOTAL
  },
  "fetchedAt": "$NOW"
}
EOF

echo "[file] wrote $USAGE_FILE"

# === Gemini CLI telemetry → gemini-usage.json ===
# Telemetry is enabled in ~/.gemini/settings.json with target=local. Each
# Gemini CLI session appends OTLP-like JSON events to telemetry.log; we sum
# input + output + cached token counts across all gemini_cli.api_response
# events. If the log doesn't exist yet, write a zero-value file (Medusa is
# the only project using Gemini CLI today; log populates on next session).

# Incremental parse: telemetry.log grows without bound (it reached 70GB once a
# full-file scan was reattempted), so we never re-read history. We track a byte
# offset in $GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE and only sum tokens from NEW bytes each run,
# carrying the cumulative total forward in $GEMINI_USAGE_FILE. First run (no
# offset file) baselines at EOF — historical pre-fix telemetry is not
# retro-counted (it was never in the headline; the manifest used a manual value).
GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE="$LOG_DIR/gemini-offset"
GEMINI_TOTAL=0
if [[ -f "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" ]]; then
  echo "[gemini] incremental parse of $GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG..."
  GEMINI_TOTAL=$(python3 -c "
import os, re, json
log = '$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG'
offset_file = '$GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE'
usage_file = '$GEMINI_USAGE_FILE'

prev_total = 0
try:
    with open(usage_file) as f:
        prev_total = int(json.load(f).get('total', 0))
except Exception:
    prev_total = 0

size = os.path.getsize(log)
try:
    with open(offset_file) as f:
        start = int(f.read().strip())
except Exception:
    start = None

if start is None:
    start = size          # first run: skip history, count only new telemetry
elif start > size:
    start = 0             # log rotated/truncated: re-scan the smaller file

delta = 0
pat = re.compile(r'(input_token_count|output_token_count|cached_content_token_count|tool_token_count)[\":=\s]+([0-9]+)')
with open(log, 'r', errors='replace') as f:
    f.seek(start)
    f.readline()          # discard a possibly-partial line at the seek boundary
    for line in f:
        for m in pat.finditer(line):
            try:
                delta += int(m.group(2))
            except ValueError:
                pass

with open(offset_file, 'w') as f:
    f.write(str(size))
print(prev_total + delta)
" 2>/dev/null)
  GEMINI_TOTAL=${GEMINI_TOTAL:-0}
  echo "[gemini] total: $GEMINI_TOTAL (cumulative, incremental)"
else
  echo "[gemini] no telemetry log yet at $GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG (will populate after next CLI session)"
fi

# Rolling size cap: keep the newest GEMINI_LOG_CAP_GB, prune oldest. Runs AFTER
# the incremental parse (which counted through EOF and saved the offset), so
# dropping already-counted old bytes loses nothing; we reset the offset to the
# post-trim EOF so the next run counts only new appends. Gemini CLI itself has
# no rotation (settings.json is just enabled/target/outfile) and re-logs its full
# ~39KB system prompt on every event, so without this the log grows unbounded.
# Hysteresis: prune only ABOVE the high watermark, and trim down to the low one —
# so a few MB over cap doesn't rewrite 10GB every run (that thrash blocked the rest
# of the script). Gemini CLI is sunsetted now, so this log is near-static; this is
# just a cheap safety backstop, not a daily chore.
GEMINI_LOG_HIGH_GB=12
GEMINI_LOG_LOW_GB=8
if [[ -f "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" ]]; then
  HIGH_BYTES=$(( GEMINI_LOG_HIGH_GB * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 ))
  LOW_BYTES=$(( GEMINI_LOG_LOW_GB * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 ))
  CUR_BYTES=$(stat -f%z "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  if (( CUR_BYTES > HIGH_BYTES )); then
    echo "[gemini] log $(( CUR_BYTES / 1073741824 ))GB > ${GEMINI_LOG_HIGH_GB}GB — pruning oldest down to ${GEMINI_LOG_LOW_GB}GB..."
    TRIM_TMP="${GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG}.trim.$$"
    if tail -c "$LOW_BYTES" "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" > "$TRIM_TMP" 2>/dev/null && mv "$TRIM_TMP" "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG"; then
      stat -f%z "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG" > "$GEMINI_OFFSET_FILE"
      echo "[gemini] trimmed to $(( $(stat -f%z "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG") / 1073741824 ))GB; offset reset to new EOF"
    else
      rm -f "$TRIM_TMP"
      echo "[gemini] WARN: trim failed; log left intact"
    fi
  fi
fi

cat > "$GEMINI_USAGE_FILE" <<EOF
{
  "total": $GEMINI_TOTAL,
  "source": "gemini-cli local telemetry",
  "telemetryLog": "$GEMINI_TELEMETRY_LOG",
  "fetchedAt": "$NOW"
}
EOF
echo "[file] wrote $GEMINI_USAGE_FILE"

# === Antigravity (agy) usage → antigravity-usage.json ===
# Cumulative lifetime tokens summed from agy's local SQLite conversation DBs,
# cached INCLUDED. Carry-forward guard: if the recomputed total drops below the
# last recorded value (e.g. agy prunes old conversation DBs), keep the previous
# higher value so the lifetime stat never regresses.
if [[ -f "$ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER" ]]; then
  echo "[antigravity] parsing $ANTIGRAVITY_CONV_DIR ..."
  AGY_JSON=$(python3 "$ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER" "$ANTIGRAVITY_CONV_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [[ -n "$AGY_JSON" ]]; then
    if python3 - "$ANTIGRAVITY_USAGE_FILE" "$NOW" "$AGY_JSON" <<'PYEOF'
import json, sys
usage_file, now, agy_json = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]
new = json.loads(agy_json)
prev_total = 0
try:
    with open(usage_file) as f:
        prev_total = int(json.load(f).get('total', 0))
except Exception:
    prev_total = 0
total = int(new.get('total', 0))
if total < prev_total:
    print(f"[antigravity] WARN: recomputed {total:,} < previous {prev_total:,} (DBs pruned?); keeping previous", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(0)
out = {
    "total": total,
    "prompt": int(new.get('prompt', 0)),
    "output": int(new.get('output', 0)),
    "cached": int(new.get('cached', 0)),
    "tool": int(new.get('tool', 0)),
    "includesCached": True,
    "source": "antigravity-cli local SQLite gen_metadata",
    "dbs": int(new.get('dbs', 0)),
    "rows": int(new.get('rows', 0)),
    "fetchedAt": now,
}
with open(usage_file, 'w') as f:
    json.dump(out, f, indent=2)
print(f"[antigravity] total {total:,} (prompt {out['prompt']:,} + output {out['output']:,} + cached {out['cached']:,} + tool {out['tool']:,})")
PYEOF
    then :; else echo "[antigravity] WARN: writer failed — leaving previous antigravity-usage.json"; fi
  else
    echo "[antigravity] parser returned nothing — skipping (no DBs yet?)"
  fi
else
  echo "[antigravity] parser not found at $ANTIGRAVITY_PARSER — skipping"
fi

cd "$PROJECT_ASSETS"
# Stage + commit BEFORE pulling: the script writes the usage files first, so a
# pull --rebase against a dirty tree errors ("unstaged changes"). Commit first,
# then rebase our commit onto the latest remote (the cloud collector pushes
# several times a day), then push. --autostash covers any stray local edits.
git add anthropic-usage.json gemini-usage.json
if [[ -f antigravity-usage.json ]]; then git add antigravity-usage.json; fi
if git diff --cached --quiet; then
  echo "[git] no changes to commit"
  git pull --rebase --autostash --quiet || true
else
  git commit -m "chore(stats): refresh AI usage agents ($NOW)"
  git pull --rebase --autostash --quiet
  git push --quiet
  echo "[git] pushed"
fi

echo "=== done ==="

content.text:
Everything's confirmed and I have a clean, low-risk design. Key facts that make this safe:

- `project-assets` has **no CLAUDE.md/CHANGELOG** of its own (only README) — so the portfolio's changelog rule doesn't apply here; I'll follow this repo's actual conventions (node:test + fixtures, README).
- Current `openai` = `api+manual` = ~59.6M, and the admin API **is** returning data (~24.6M of API-platform usage). Codex is ChatGPT-auth → **not** in that number → adding ~14.5M is genuinely additive.
- `tokens.mjs` is untested today — I'll add `tokens.test.mjs` and refactor agent-file reads to be fixture-injectable so the new wiring is actually covered.

Let me set up the branch and file the tracking issue, then implement.

input.command:
cd /Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets
git checkout -b feat/codex-token-source 2>&1 | tail -1
echo "---issue---"
gh issue create \
  --title "[feature] Count OpenAI Codex CLI tokens (ccusage codex) as an additive openai source" \
  --body "## What
Wire OpenAI Codex CLI usage into the token collector as an additive \`agent\` contribution to the \`openai\` provider, via \`ccusage codex\`.

## Why
Codex usage is currently counted as **zero** (~14.5M tokens on Cursatory across 4 months, unseen). Codex CLI authenticates via ChatGPT login (\`auth_mode=chatgpt\`), so its tokens are billed through the subscription and do **not** appear in the OpenAI admin-API usage report — they are genuinely additive, no double-count. This mirrors how \`ccusage\` already feeds the \`anthropic\` line.

## Scope
- \`local-agent/refresh.sh\`: run \`ccusage codex\` (Cursatory + habitat) → write \`codex-usage.json\` (carry-forward guard, non-fatal).
- \`scripts/lib/tokens.mjs\`: read \`codex-usage.json\` → fold into \`breakdown.openai\` as \`agent\`; source becomes \`api+agent+manual\`.
- \`scripts/lib/tokens.test.mjs\`: new — cover the additive openai path.

## Out of scope (deferred)
- **OpenClaw**: routes through Monad; its \`tokens.total\` already counts these (double-count). Guarded by \`OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS=[]\`.
- **Copilot CLI**: no local logs present on the collector machines; stays prorated." 2>&1 | tail -2

input.old_string:
echo "[file] wrote $USAGE_FILE"

# === Gemini CLI telemetry → gemini-usage.json ===
input.new_string:
echo "[file] wrote $USAGE_FILE"

# === OpenAI Codex CLI usage → codex-usage.json ===
# Codex CLI authenticates via ChatGPT login (auth_mode=chatgpt), so its tokens are
# billed through the ChatGPT subscription, NOT the OpenAI API platform — they do
# NOT appear in the OpenAI admin-API usage report. The collector therefore folds
# this in as an additive 'agent' contribution to the openai provider (mirrors how
# ccusage feeds anthropic). Read via `ccusage codex`, which ships in ccusage@latest
# (the globally-installed 20.0.14 lacks the subcommand), so use npx @latest on both
# machines. Non-fatal throughout: Codex is a small line and must never block the
# anthropic/gemini/antigravity push below.
echo "[codex/cursatory] running ccusage codex..."
CODEX_CURSATORY=$(npx -y ccusage@latest codex monthly --json 2>/dev/null | parse_total) || CODEX_CURSATORY=0
CODEX_CURSATORY=${CODEX_CURSATORY:-0}
echo "[codex/cursatory] total: $CODEX_CURSATORY"

echo "[codex/habitat] running ccusage codex via SSH..."
CODEX_HABITAT=$(ssh -o ConnectTimeout=15 -o BatchMode=yes habitat \
  'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; npx -y ccusage@latest codex monthly --json' 2>/dev/null \
  | parse_total) || CODEX_HABITAT=0
CODEX_HABITAT=${CODEX_HABITAT:-0}
echo "[codex/habitat] total: $CODEX_HABITAT"

CODEX_TOTAL=$((CODEX_CURSATORY + CODEX_HABITAT))
echo "[codex/combined] total: $CODEX_TOTAL"

# Carry-forward guard: if this run regressed below the last recorded value (a
# machine unreachable, or Codex session logs pruned), leave the existing file
# untouched so the additive openai line never drops. Only rewrite on >= previous.
CODEX_PREV=0
if [[ -f "$CODEX_USAGE_FILE" ]]; then
  CODEX_PREV=$(python3 -c "import json;print(json.load(open('$CODEX_USAGE_FILE')).get('total',0))" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
fi
if [[ "$CODEX_TOTAL" -lt "$CODEX_PREV" ]]; then
  echo "[codex] WARN: new total $CODEX_TOTAL < previous $CODEX_PREV — keeping previous file"
else
  cat > "$CODEX_USAGE_FILE" <<EOF
{
  "total": $CODEX_TOTAL,
  "provider": "openai",
  "source": "codex-cli (ccusage codex; ChatGPT-auth, excluded from OpenAI admin API)",
  "byMachine": {
    "cursatory": $CODEX_CURSATORY,
    "habitat": $CODEX_HABITAT
  },
  "fetchedAt": "$NOW"
}
EOF
  echo "[file] wrote $CODEX_USAGE_FILE"
fi

# === Gemini CLI telemetry → gemini-usage.json ===

content.text: Now the independent Critic review on the diff (it sees only the changes, not my reasoning).

input.description: Independent critic review of diff
input.prompt:
You are an independent Critic reviewing a code change in the repo at `/Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets`. You did NOT write this and have no access to the author's reasoning — judge only the code.

**Context:** This is a daily stats collector. `scripts/lib/tokens.mjs::aggregateTokens()` aggregates AI token usage across providers (anthropic, openai, copilot, cursor, gemini, antigravity). Each provider's number = sum of up to 4 additive source types: admin-API (`api`), local-agent JSON files (`agent`), static `manual` entries, and `prorated` formulas. A hard rule in the file header: **never count the same usage in two source types.**

**The change** adds OpenAI Codex CLI usage as a new `agent` contribution to the `openai` provider:
- `local-agent/refresh.sh` runs `ccusage codex` on Cursatory + habitat and writes `codex-usage.json`.
- `tokens.mjs` reads `codex-usage.json` and folds its `total` into `breakdown.openai` (now `oaiApi + oaiAgent + oaiManual`), with a `sourceMix()` helper and a new `cfg.agentDir` param for test fixtures.
- `scripts/lib/tokens.test.mjs` is new.
- `codex-usage.json` committed with total 14514416.

The author's justification for no-double-count: Codex CLI here uses `auth_mode=chatgpt` (ChatGPT login), so its tokens are billed via subscription and do NOT appear in the OpenAI admin-API usage report (`/v1/organization/usage/completions`), making it additive to the `api` source.

**Review the staged diff:** run `cd /Users/jasonvaughan/code/project-assets && git --no-pager diff --cached` and read `scripts/lib/tokens.mjs` and `local-agent/refresh.sh` in full.

Check specifically for:
1. **Double-count risk** — is the ChatGPT-auth justification sound? Any scenario where Codex tokens WOULD also land in the admin-API number, or in another provider line? What if auth_mode changes to apikey later — does anything guard that?
2. **Correctness** — the `cfg.agentDir` refactor: does production behavior stay identical (default REPO_ROOT)? Did removing the named wrapper functions break any other caller? grep for `readAnthropicAgentTotal`/`readGeminiAgentTotal`/`readAntigravityAgentTotal` usages elsewhere.
3. **refresh.sh robustness** — `set -euo pipefail` interaction with the new block; the carry-forward guard; the `parse_total` reuse for codex JSON shape; non-fatal failure modes; the `byMachine` on regression (file left untouched — is that consistent?).
4. **Test coverage gaps** — what's NOT tested that should be.
5. **Scope creep** — anything beyond "add Codex as openai agent source."
6. **Doc parity** — header JSDoc vs actual returned shape.

Report findings classified BLOCKER / MAJOR / MINOR / NIT with file:line and a concrete fix for each. If you find nothing material, say so plainly. Be skeptical and specific; do not rubber-stamp.

content.text:
All 68 tests pass; codex-usage.json tracking is consistent with the other usage files. I have enough to write the review.

## Independent Critic Review — Codex token source

**Verdict:** Solid, well-tested change. Production behavior is preserved, the refactor is clean, and the core no-double-count argument holds for the stated auth mode. No BLOCKERs. The findings below are real but bounded — the most important is an unguarded future-failure mode (auth_mode drift), not a bug today.

### MAJOR

**M1 — No guard against `auth_mode=apikey` drift; the double-count is silent if it happens.**
`local-agent/refresh.sh:115-124` runs `ccusage codex monthly` and folds the result into the `openai` line as an additive `agent` source. The entire no-double-count guarantee rests on Codex running under `auth_mode=chatgpt` (subscription-billed, invisible to `/v1/organization/usage/completions`). That premise is correct *today*, but nothing enforces it:

- If the user ever runs `codex login --api-key` (or sets `OPENAI_API_KEY` and Codex falls back to API auth), those sessions **do** bill the API platform and **do** appear in `fetchOpenAITokens()` (`tokens.mjs:77-111`). `ccusage codex` reads local session logs regardless of auth mode, so the same tokens would land in both `oaiApi` and `oaiAgent` → `breakdown.openai = oaiApi + oaiAgent + oaiManual` double-counts them.
- `parse_total` (`refresh.sh:39-54`) sums `monthly[].totalTokens` across **all** months/sessions with no filter on auth mode.

The justification is sound but it's a *convention*, not a *contract* — exactly the failure class the file header warns against. Concrete fix: in the codex block, filter to ChatGPT-auth sessions if `ccusage codex` exposes that field, or at minimum assert it. If ccusage can't distinguish, add a loud comment + a `WISHLIST`/issue note that switching Codex to API auth requires removing this agent source. A cheap mechanical guard: skip the codex write when `OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY`/`OPENAI_API_KEY` indicates the org is also reporting Codex via the API. At present I'd accept this shipping with an explicit issue filed documenting the drift hazard.

### MINOR

**m1 — `parse_total` schema reuse for `ccusage codex` is unverified.** `refresh.sh:116,122` pipe `ccusage codex monthly --json` through the same `parse_total` that sums `monthly[].totalTokens` (`refresh.sh:50`). I could not confirm live that `ccusage codex` emits the identical `{monthly:[{totalTokens}]}` shape (no codex sessions on this box; command returned empty). If codex output nests differently (e.g. `totalTokens` under a sub-key, or a different top array), `parse_total` silently returns 0 and the carry-forward guard then *keeps the stale committed value forever* — the worst outcome because it looks healthy. The committed `total: 14514416` suggests it parsed on cursatory at least once, so this is likely fine, but it should be confirmed against the actual `ccusage codex --json` schema before relying on the daily refresh. Fix: verify the schema once, and consider having `parse_total` log to stderr when it finds the `{` but sums to 0 (distinguish "no usage" from "shape mismatch").

**m2 — `agentMeta.openai` shape diverges and `antigravity` is still absent from `agentMeta`.** `tokens.mjs:279-281` adds `agentMeta.openai = { byMachine, fetchedAt }` (machine-keyed, like anthropic), while `gemini` uses `{ source, fetchedAt }`. That's internally consistent (codex is per-machine like anthropic). But note `agentMeta` still omits `antigravity` entirely (pre-existing gap, not introduced here) — so `agentMeta` now covers anthropic/openai/gemini but not antigravity, which is asymmetric. Out of scope to fix, but worth a one-line issue.

**m3 — Header JSDoc `agentMeta` example is stale.** `tokens.mjs:30` still reads `agentMeta: { anthropic: { byMachine, fetchedAt }, gemini: {...} }` — it doesn't mention the new `openai` key the change adds at line 279. Doc-parity nit on the very file whose header preaches the no-double-count rule. Fix: update the example to `{ anthropic, openai, gemini }`.

### NIT

**n1 — Asymmetric regression guard between anthropic and codex blocks (intentional but undocumented divergence).** The anthropic block uses a hard `exit 1` on zero and a **5%-tolerance** threshold (`refresh.sh:71-89`); the codex block uses a strict `< previous` carry-forward (`refresh.sh:137`) and never aborts. That's defensible (codex is non-fatal, must not block the push), and the strict `<` is actually *safer* than the 5% band for a never-regress stat. The header comment at `refresh.sh:113-114` explains the non-fatal intent. No change needed; just flagging that the two guards now differ in kind and a future reader shouldn't "harmonize" them blindly.

**n2 — `byMachine` left untouched on codex regression is consistent.** Verified: on regression the whole file (including `byMachine`) is left intact (`refresh.sh:137-138`), matching the antigravity carry-forward pattern. Correct and consistent — the `total` and its `byMachine` breakdown stay in sync because neither is rewritten. No issue.

### Verified clean
- **Production parity of the `agentDir` refactor:** confirmed. `collect-stats.mjs:269-270` passes `cfg.tokens` with no `agentDir`, so `cfg.agentDir || REPO_ROOT` (`tokens.mjs:174`) resolves to the same absolute paths the old module-level constants used. Behavior is byte-identical in prod.
- **No broken callers:** the removed `readAnthropicAgentTotal`/`readGeminiAgentTotal`/`readAntigravityAgentTotal` wrappers were module-private and had zero external references (grepped repo-wide). Only `aggregateTokens` is imported by `collect-stats.mjs`. Newly-exported `readAgentFile`/`sourceMix` are used only by the test.
- **Tests:** full suite green (68 pass). The two new `aggregateTokens` tests correctly isolate by clearing `*_ADMIN_KEY` env so `api=0`, and assert both the additive math (`14M + 1M manual = 15M`, `sources=agent+manual`) and the absent-file path (`sources=manual`, `agentMeta.openai=null`).
- **`set -euo pipefail` interaction:** the new codex commands use `|| CODEX_*=0` + `${VAR:-0}` belt-and-suspenders, and `$(...) | parse_total` won't trip `pipefail` because the failure is absorbed by the `||`. Safe.

### Test coverage gaps (for M1/m1)
The tests prove codex feeds `openai` additively but **never exercise the api+agent combination** — the exact path where the M1 double-count would manifest. There's no test asserting `breakdown.openai = oaiApi + oaiAgent + oaiManual` when `oai.ok` is true. Recommend adding a test that mocks/forces a non-zero `oaiApi` (or refactors `fetchOpenAITokens` to be injectable) and asserts the three-way sum + `sources.openai === 'api+agent+manual'`. As-is, the api branch of the new line `238` is untested.

### Scope
No scope creep. Everything maps to "add Codex as an openai agent source" plus the minimal `cfg.agentDir` test-seam and the `sourceMix` extraction (justified DRY — collapses four near-identical source-label ladders, though note only anthropic+openai were converted; gemini/antigravity ladders at `tokens.mjs:196-203` were left in the old form, a small consistency miss but not a regression).

**Recommended before merge:** file an issue capturing M1 (auth_mode drift hazard), verify m1 (codex JSON schema) on a machine with real Codex sessions, and fix the m3 doc nit. M1's issue can ship alongside rather than block.

input.old_string:
# === OpenAI Codex CLI usage → codex-usage.json ===
# Codex CLI authenticates via ChatGPT login (auth_mode=chatgpt), so its tokens are
# billed through the ChatGPT subscription, NOT the OpenAI API platform — they do
# NOT appear in the OpenAI admin-API usage report. The collector therefore folds
# this in as an additive 'agent' contribution to the openai provider (mirrors how
# ccusage feeds anthropic). Read via `ccusage codex`, which ships in ccusage@latest
# (the globally-installed 20.0.14 lacks the subcommand), so use npx @latest on both
# machines. Non-fatal throughout: Codex is a small line and must never block the
# anthropic/gemini/antigravity push below.
echo "[codex/cursatory] running ccusage codex..."
CODEX_CURSATORY=$(npx -y ccusage@latest codex monthly --json 2>/dev/null | parse_total) || CODEX_CURSATORY=0
CODEX_CURSATORY=${CODEX_CURSATORY:-0}
echo "[codex/cursatory] total: $CODEX_CURSATORY"

echo "[codex/habitat] running ccusage codex via SSH..."
CODEX_HABITAT=$(ssh -o ConnectTimeout=15 -o BatchMode=yes habitat \
  'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; npx -y ccusage@latest codex monthly --json' 2>/dev/null \
  | parse_total) || CODEX_HABITAT=0
CODEX_HABITAT=${CODEX_HABITAT:-0}
echo "[codex/habitat] total: $CODEX_HABITAT"
input.new_string:
# === OpenAI Codex CLI usage → codex-usage.json ===
# Codex CLI authenticates via ChatGPT login (auth_mode=chatgpt), so its tokens are
# billed through the ChatGPT subscription, NOT the OpenAI API platform — they do
# NOT appear in the OpenAI admin-API usage report. The collector therefore folds
# this in as an additive 'agent' contribution to the openai provider (mirrors how
# ccusage feeds anthropic). Read via `ccusage codex`, which ships in ccusage@latest
# (the globally-installed 20.0.14 lacks the subcommand), so use npx @latest on both
# machines. Non-fatal throughout: Codex is a small line and must never block the
# anthropic/gemini/antigravity push below.
#
# CONTRACT (not just convention): only count a machine's Codex usage when its
# auth_mode == "chatgpt". API-key-auth Codex sessions DO hit the OpenAI API
# platform and are already captured by tokens.mjs::fetchOpenAITokens(), so counting
# them here too would double-count. We check auth_mode per machine and zero the
# contribution otherwise — so flipping Codex to API auth can never silently
# double-count; the admin API just picks it up instead.

# Prints "chatgpt" iff the given auth.json has auth_mode == chatgpt, else "".
codex_auth_mode() {
  python3 -c "import json,sys
try:
    print(json.load(open(sys.argv[1])).get('auth_mode',''))
except Exception:
    print('')" "$1" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

echo "[codex/cursatory] running ccusage codex..."
CODEX_CURSATORY=0
if [[ "$(codex_auth_mode "$HOME/.codex/auth.json")" == "chatgpt" ]]; then
  CODEX_CURSATORY=$(npx -y ccusage@latest codex monthly --json 2>/dev/null | parse_total) || CODEX_CURSATORY=0
  CODEX_CURSATORY=${CODEX_CURSATORY:-0}
else
  echo "[codex/cursatory] auth_mode != chatgpt (or no Codex) — skipping to avoid admin-API double-count"
fi
echo "[codex/cursatory] total: $CODEX_CURSATORY"

echo "[codex/habitat] checking Codex auth_mode via SSH..."
HABITAT_CODEX_AUTH=$(ssh -o ConnectTimeout=15 -o BatchMode=yes habitat \
  'python3 -c "import json,os
try:
    print(json.load(open(os.path.expanduser(\"~/.codex/auth.json\"))).get(\"auth_mode\",\"\"))
except Exception:
    print(\"\")"' 2>/dev/null) || HABITAT_CODEX_AUTH=""
CODEX_HABITAT=0
if [[ "$HABITAT_CODEX_AUTH" == "chatgpt" ]]; then
  echo "[codex/habitat] running ccusage codex via SSH..."
  CODEX_HABITAT=$(ssh -o ConnectTimeout=15 -o BatchMode=yes habitat \
    'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"; npx -y ccusage@latest codex monthly --json' 2>/dev/null \
    | parse_total) || CODEX_HABITAT=0
  CODEX_HABITAT=${CODEX_HABITAT:-0}
else
  echo "[codex/habitat] auth_mode='$HABITAT_CODEX_AUTH' != chatgpt (or no Codex/unreachable) — skipping"
fi
echo "[codex/habitat] total: $CODEX_HABITAT"

gh pr create --title "Count Codex CLI tokens as an additive openai source" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What
Wire OpenAI Codex CLI usage into the token collector via `ccusage codex`, contributing to the **`openai`** provider line as a local `agent` source — symmetric with how `ccusage` already feeds `anthropic`.

Before: Codex counted as **zero** (~14.5M tokens unseen on Cursatory across 4 months).
After: `openai = api + agent(codex) + manual` → in CI ≈ **74M** (was 59.6M); source `api+agent+manual`.

## Why
Codex CLI here authenticates via **ChatGPT login** (`auth_mode=chatgpt`), so its tokens are billed through the subscription and do **not** appear in the OpenAI admin-API usage report — genuinely additive, no double-count.

`refresh.sh` enforces this as a **contract, not a convention**: it only counts a machine's Codex usage when `auth_mode == "chatgpt"`. If Codex is ever switched to API-key auth, those tokens land in the admin-API number instead and the agent source zeroes out — so it can never silently double-count.

**Deferred (separate, by design):** OpenClaw routes through Monad (`tokens.total` already counts it — guarded by `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS=[]`); Copilot CLI has no local logs on the collector machines (stays prorated).

## Changes
- `local-agent/refresh.sh` — run `ccusage codex` on Cursatory + habitat (auth-mode guarded, carry-forward guard, non-fatal) → `codex-usage.json`.
- `scripts/lib/tokens.mjs` — read `codex-usage.json` into `breakdown.openai`; extract `sourceMix()` helper; add `cfg.agentDir` test seam (production unchanged: defaults to `REPO_ROOT`).
- `scripts/lib/tokens.test.mjs` — **new**; first tests for `tokens.mjs` (sourceMix, readAgentFile, additive openai path).
- `codex-usage.json` — seeded with the real Cursatory total.

## Test plan
- `node --test scripts/lib/*.test.mjs` → **68 pass / 0 fail** (16 new).
- `bash -n local-agent/refresh.sh` → clean.
- Live `aggregateTokens()` against real repo files: `openai` = 49.5M locally (35M manual + 14.5M codex agent), source `agent+manual`; `api+agent+manual` in CI where the admin key is present.
- Independent Critic review run on the diff; its MAJOR (auth_mode drift) is fixed here, remaining MINORs tracked in #27.

Fixes #26. Follow-ups: #27.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
Done. Here's where things landed.

## What I shipped

**The fix:** Codex CLI usage was being counted as **zero** — ~14.5M real tokens invisible. Now wired into the collector via `ccusage codex`, folded into the **`openai`** line as an `agent` source (symmetric with how ccusage already feeds `anthropic`).

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `openai` breakdown | 59.6M (`api+manual`) | ~74M in CI (`api+agent+manual`) |
| Codex tokens | invisible (0) | counted (14.5M+) |

**Why it's safe (no double-count):** Your Codex auth is `auth_mode=chatgpt` → billed via the ChatGPT subscription, so those tokens never appear in the OpenAI admin-API number. And I made that a **contract, not a convention**: `refresh.sh` only counts a machine's Codex if `auth_mode == "chatgpt"` — flip Codex to an API key someday and it auto-zeroes out (the admin API picks it up instead). This was the Critic's one MAJOR finding, fixed before merge.

**What I deliberately did *not* touch:**
- **OpenClaw** — routes through Monad, whose `tokens.total` already counts it (your `OPENCLAW_AGENT_STATS_URLS=[]` guard). Adding it would double-count.
- **Copilot** — no CLI logs exist on your collector machines, so it stays prorated.

## Process artifacts
- Issue **#26** (the fix) → **PR #28** (`Fixes #26`), CI running (`node-test` pending).
- Issue **#27** — deferred Critic minors (test the `api+agent` path via injectable fetchers, `agentMeta` antigravity gap, sourceMix consistency).
- **68 tests pass** (16 new — `tokens.mjs` had *zero* tests before; now covered).
- Independent Critic review run on the diff.

**PR #28 is left for you to review/merge** — it's a feature that changes a displayed number, so per your rules I didn't auto-merge. Once CI is green it's ready.

One thing worth flagging: the live value will only flow to the portfolio after the daily collector runs (06:00 UTC) reading the committed `codex-usage.json` — or you can trigger the collect workflow manually if you want to see the bump sooner.
